messages.content: You are a rigorous structured-judgment engine. Judge only the stated attribute. Use the item text, not outside popularity. Return strict JSON only. Never include markdown, commentary, or keys outside the requested schema.

Case: first_user_path
Attribute maintenance_cost: how cheap this is to maintain as the code and models evolve

A (when_not_to_use_cardinal): A prominent section showing cases where scalar metrics, deterministic sorting, or whole-list ranking are cheaper and more appropriate.

B (receipt_debugging_guide): A troubleshooting guide for cache misses, model refusals, cost estimates, non-convergence, and inconsistent pairwise judgments.

Task: Which item has more of this attribute? Choose one item ID. Output JSON exactly: {"higher_id":"item id","confidence":0.0}.
